_comment: Harness-neutral vocabulary layer (plan scenario 2; arch note 'How thin the
metalanguage can be'). A capability is named ONCE, neutrally; each harness's
manifest resolves it to that harness's concrete primitive. The generator reads
this lookup to decide HOW a capability is realized in the target adapter.
Slice 1 ships only the 'claude' column populated. The 'opencode' column is
present as documented intent (sourced from doc/stack-notes.md) so a later slice
plugs OpenCode in by filling primitives + adding an opencode adapter manifest,
WITHOUT re-carving any neutral body. The bodies in src/ are already harness-
For Claude (the reference harness) every primitive is realized by an artifact
that already lives in the Claude manifest (frontmatter blocks, settings.json),
so the slice-1 substitutions are effectively identity: the generator emits the
verbatim Claude artifact. The structure — not a near-identity Claude mapping —
is what lets a non-identity harness slot in later.

subagent-definition.description: How a pm-* subagent's metadata (name, description, tool grant) is declared alongside its neutral body.

claude.realized_by: claude/frontmatter/<name>.fm (verbatim per-agent block: name + description + comma-list tools)

opencode.realized_by: opencode/frontmatter/<name>.fm (description + mode: subagent + tools OBJECT map; model/permission deferred — cross-model pins are a later slice)

command-definition.description: How a /pm-* command body is declared. On Claude the command file is pure body, no frontmatter.

claude.realized_by: the neutral command body, emitted verbatim (no frontmatter)

opencode.realized_by: .opencode/command/<name>.md (neutral body emitted verbatim; command bodies MAY use @file inline — an asymmetry the generator does NOT over-apply to agent bodies)

deny-list-enforcement.description: Block a class-1 role-duplicator spawn/skill-load; guard boundary/destructive ops.

opencode.realized_by: .opencode/ plugin: tool.execute.before throws to deny / mutates output.args to rewrite
opencode.status: doc-cited (unverified) — Spike B (#5894) gates subagent containment

route-reminder.description: Inject the protocol route on change-intent prompts.

opencode.realized_by: baked into AGENTS.md / instructions (always-on, NOT per-prompt — no UserPromptSubmit-equivalent hook)

always-on-core-import.description: Make the always-on core (WORKFLOW.md) load for every session.

claude.realized_by: the @WORKFLOW.md import in CLAUDE.md (out of slice-1 scope — adapter slice covers .claude/ only)

opencode.realized_by: .opencode/opencode.json instructions[] -> .ai-pm/tooling/WORKFLOW.md (submodule-relative path); no @-import inside an instruction file

structured-question-tool.description: Surface a substantive product fork as a structured choice, not free prose.

opencode.realized_by: the OpenCode runtime `question` tool (no per-harness adapter file — the neutral agent bodies name the structured-question capability harness-agnostically); available to the slice-2 .opencode/ subagents
opencode.status: BUILT in slice 2 (runtime tool; no emitted artifact)

opencode.realized_by: the OpenCode runtime `skill` tool (no per-harness adapter file — the neutral bodies name skill-invocation harness-agnostically); available to the slice-2 .opencode/ subagents (e.g. pm-coder/pm-stack-researcher carry skill: true)
opencode.status: BUILT in slice 2 (runtime tool; no emitted artifact)
